target: data/sections.json: add domain-count volatility note (ISS-1698, part2b), fix 3.6x wording from ambiguous 'outside' to precise 'threshold' (ISS-1715, part4), add SG literature citation to storm corollary paragraph (ISS-1966, part6)

patches.target_locator: part2b.html — Monitor Avoidance Pattern section, sentence beginning '39 domains, polling NOAA, USGS, HeartMath, and OpenSky'
patches.current_text: 39 domains, polling NOAA, USGS, HeartMath, and OpenSky every five minutes
patches.proposed_text: 39 domains (count verified 2026-04-27; grew from 28 to 39 in under two weeks and may continue to drift), polling NOAA, USGS, HeartMath, and OpenSky every five minutes
patches.rationale: ISS-1698: the 39-domain count rose from 28 to 39 in approximately ten days, making it a volatile snapshot. Adding the timestamp and drift note prevents this from reading as a stable structural count. The curmudgeon recommended an optional footnote; inline parenthetical is lighter and sufficient for a minor note.

patches.target_locator: part4.html — §4.2.2 TLDR paragraph (ps-tldr class), final clause 'already sits 3.6× outside its ±3 nT globe window'
patches.current_text: the dome’s own BOU-2017 baseline already sits 3.6× outside its ±3 nT “globe” window.
patches.proposed_text: the dome’s own BOU-2017 baseline already shows a deviation 3.6× the ±3 nT “globe” threshold.
patches.rationale: ISS-1715: '3.6× outside' is ambiguous — it could mean '3.6 units beyond' or '3.6 times the threshold'. The precise meaning is that the observed deviation (≈10.8 nT) is 3.6 times the ±3 nT threshold. Replacing 'sits X× outside its threshold' with 'shows a deviation X× the threshold' removes the ambiguity without changing the mathematical content.

patches.target_locator: part6.html — §6.14 storm corollary paragraph, sentence 'The published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses...'
patches.current_text: The published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses tracking storm magnetic perturbations through κ; the long-running SG records would have flagged such signals, and dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them.
patches.proposed_text: The published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses tracking storm magnetic perturbations through κ (see e.g. Hinderer & Crossley 2004; Crossley et al. 2013 — Global Geodynamics Project reviews reporting on SG behavior across geomagnetic events); the long-running SG records would have flagged such signals, and dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them.
patches.rationale: ISS-1966: the storm-corollary paragraph asserted the absence of storm-correlated SG signals without citing the literature that supports this. Adding GGP review references (Hinderer & Crossley 2004; Crossley et al. 2013) gives a verifiable basis: these are the principal reviews of the Global Geodynamics Project superconducting gravimeter network, which would be the primary source for any storm-gravity coupling signal if it existed.
